label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out which of the options is wrong based on the given news article. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand all the details. The first paragraph explains that the Columbia space shuttle broke up over Texas, resulting in the deaths of all seven astronauts. NASA started an investigation but couldn't determine the exact cause yet. The breakup happened 16 minutes before landing, spreading debris, possibly toxic, over Texas and neighboring states. The next paragraph mentions that dramatic TV images showed several white trails after the shuttle broke apart. It's noted that this was 17 years almost to the day since the Challenger explosion on January 28, 1986. Then, the article states that take-off and re-entry are the most dangerous parts of a space mission. It also says that in 42 years of US human space flight, there had never been an accident during descent to Earth or landing. The Challenger exploded during take-off. Rescue teams were searching for remains of the crew, including Col. Ilan Ramon, the first Israeli on the shuttle. There were warnings about potential toxic debris from the 120-mile-long corridor. The sheriff mentioned that they weren't ready to confirm finding human remains yet, but some people reported finding crew remains. President Bush stated there were no survivors and that the space program would continue. Now, let's look at the options: (A) will give up the space program because of the accident. (B) NASA hasn't found the cause of the Columbia disaster. (C) Before the Columbia disaster, no shuttles had exploded in the course of landing. (D) Take-off and re-entry into Earth's atmosphere are the most dangerous parts of a space mission. Let's check each one against the article. Option A: The article says "America's space program will go on," meaning they won't give it up. So option A is wrong, which makes it the correct answer to the question (since the question asks which is wrong). Option B: The article says NASA launched an investigation and it was too early to nail down a precise cause, so this is correct.
